findings.justification: When $regular_price or $sale_price is non-numeric, the raw string value is interpolated directly into the HTML output inside <del> and <ins> tags without any escaping, enabling stored XSS if callers pass attacker-controlled non-numeric price strings.
Non-numeric $from and $to values are concatenated unescaped into the price range HTML via sprintf, so attacker-controlled non-numeric price values reach the DOM without sanitization.

wp_kses_post is used to sanitize price decimal/thousand separator settings, which allows a broad HTML tag allowlist for a field that should only ever contain a single punctuation character, meaning stored HTML tags will survive into rendered output.

The price_format argument, which is used directly as a sprintf format string in HTML construction, is fully controllable via the wc_price_args filter hook, meaning any hooked plugin can inject arbitrary format strings or HTML into price output.

html_entity_decode() is called on the raw coupon code value before the wp_kses sanitization pipeline, which can cause encoded XSS payloads to decode into executable form before kses evaluation, potentially surviving if the kses allowlist is broad enough.
